programs.name: Medicaid Long-Term Care Services Program
programs.what_it_does: Helps seniors pay for long-term care services including nursing facility care, intermediate care facilities, and home and community-based services. Covers some or all costs for eligible individuals.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as requiring nursing home level of care and financially eligible for Medicaid
programs.benefit_value: Covers long-term care costs for eligible beneficiaries

programs.name: Community Choices Waiver (CCW)
programs.what_it_does: Provides long-term care benefits to seniors requiring a nursing facility level of care, allowing them to live in their own home, a loved one's home, assisted living, or adult foster care home without covering room and board. Offers home and community-based services like personal assistance and adult day health care to avoid institutionalization.[1][4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Louisiana seniors needing nursing home level of care, eligible for Medicaid, living in community settings.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Louisiana's specific Medicaid HCBS waiver tailored for seniors to receive nursing facility-equivalent care at home or in assisted living, distinct from standard federal Medicaid by state-specific implementation and service combinations.[1][6]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by individual plan; covers personal care, adult day health, and other HCBS services at no cost to eligible Medicaid recipients beyond standard coverage.[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive care coordination and services for seniors meeting nursing home level of care requirements. Available in specific service areas.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as requiring nursing home level of care, financially eligible for Medicaid, living in Baton Rouge, Greater New Orleans, or Lafayette service areas
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive care services for eligible seniors

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: State programs that help pay Medicare Part A and Part B premiums for eligible low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A and B premiums; SLMB covers Part B premiums; QI also covers Part B premiums with higher income limits.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income thresholds
programs.benefit_value: Covers Medicare premiums for eligible beneficiaries

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits to low-income households to purchase food needed for good health. Benefits account for part of household food budgets.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households meeting income and resource requirements
programs.benefit_value: Monthly food assistance benefits (amount varies by household size)

programs.name: Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP) - Senior Box Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides boxes of nutritious food each month to low-income seniors to improve diet and health. More than 30,000 seniors receive boxes monthly at sites throughout Louisiana.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ with income at or below 150% of Federal Poverty Guidelines
programs.benefit_value: Monthly food box provided at no cost

programs.name: State Personal Assistance Services (SPAS) Program
programs.what_it_does: Delivers personal assistance services to adults with significant disabilities to enable community living and prevent institutional care. Funded and administered directly by the Louisiana Department of Health Office of Aging and Adult Services.[4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Louisiana residents aged 65+ or disabled adults 21+, including those in nursing homes transitioning to community, those at risk of nursing home admission within 120 days, or with a primary caregiver aged 70+ or disabled.[4]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded (non-Medicaid waiver) program unique to Louisiana providing personal care services under state plan authority, allowing dual eligibility with certain waivers like Adult Day Health Care.[4][6]
programs.benefit_value: Personal assistance hours based on assessed needs; no cost for eligible participants.[6]

programs.name: Adult Day Health Care Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Provides adult day health care services to support seniors in community settings, preventing or delaying nursing home placement. Can be used alongside LT-PCS for comprehensive non-institutional care.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Louisiana seniors eligible for Medicaid long-term care services requiring nursing facility level of care.[1][4]
programs.whats_unique: Louisiana-specific Medicaid HCBS waiver offering adult day health care as a core service, distinct in its state-tailored eligibility and integration with other LA programs.[1][9]
programs.benefit_value: Adult day health care services covered; value depends on care plan, no participant cost for eligibles.[1]

programs.name: Caregiver Voucher Program
programs.what_it_does: Supplies vouchers for services to family caregivers or providers caring for elderly individuals with Alzheimer's or related conditions at home. Helps caregivers deliver optimal homecare through varied support options.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Eligible clients or participants aged 60+ in St. James Parish, LA, with family caregivers addressing Alzheimer's care needs.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Parish-level (St. James) state and local government-funded voucher program specifically for Alzheimer's caregivers, not a standard federal offering and varying by LA locality.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers for caregiver services; amount based on assessed needs, funded by GOEA and local government.[2]
